The first science fiction fanzine, *[The Comet](/wiki/The_Comet_(fanzine) "The Comet (fanzine)")*, was published in 1930 by the Science Correspondence Club in Chicago, Illinois.[[272]](#cite_note-first_fanzine-272)[[273]](#cite_note-273) As of 2025, one of the best known fanzines is *[Ansible](/wiki/Ansible_(magazine) "Ansible (magazine)")*, edited by [David Langford](/wiki/David_Langford "David Langford"), winner of numerous [Hugo awards](/wiki/Hugo_Award "Hugo Award").[[274]](#cite_note-274)[[275]](#cite_note-275) Other notable fanzines to win one or more Hugo awards include *[File 770](/wiki/File_770 "File 770")*, *[Mimosa](/wiki/Mimosa_(magazine) "Mimosa (magazine)")*, and *[Plokta](/wiki/Plokta "Plokta")*.[[276]](#cite_note-The_Hugo_Awards-2007-276) Artists working for fanzines have often risen to prominence in the field, including [Brad W. Foster](/wiki/Brad_W._Foster "Brad W. Foster"), [Teddy Harvia](/wiki/Teddy_Harvia "Teddy Harvia"), and Joe Mayhew; the Hugo Awards include a category for [Best Fan Artists](/wiki/Hugo_Award_for_Best_Fan_Artist "Hugo Award for Best Fan Artist").[[276]](#cite_note-The_Hugo_Awards-2007-276)

## Elements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Science_fiction&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Elements")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/Future_Birthplace_of_Captain_James_T_Kirk.jpg/250px-Future_Birthplace_of_Captain_James_T_Kirk.jpg)](/wiki/File:Future_Birthplace_of_Captain_James_T_Kirk.jpg)

Plaque in [Riverside, Iowa](/wiki/Riverside,_Iowa "Riverside, Iowa"), to honor the "future birth" of *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek "Star Trek")*'s character [James T. Kirk](/wiki/James_T._Kirk "James T. Kirk")

Science fiction elements can include the following:

* Temporal settings in the future or in [alternative histories](/wiki/Alternative_histories "Alternative histories");[[277]](#cite_note-counterfact-277)
* Predicted or speculative technology such as [brain-computer interface](/wiki/Brain-computer_interface "Brain-computer interface"), [bio-engineering](/wiki/Bio-engineering "Bio-engineering"), [superintelligent](/wiki/Superintelligent "Superintelligent") [computers](/wiki/Computer "Computer"), [robots](/wiki/Robot "Robot"), [ray guns](/wiki/Ray_guns "Ray guns"), and [advanced weapons](/wiki/Weapons_in_science_fiction "Weapons in science fiction");[[278]](#cite_note-GW-278)[[279]](#cite_note-How_to_Write_Science_Fiction_and_Fantasy-279)
* [Space travel](/wiki/Space_travel_in_science_fiction "Space travel in science fiction"), or settings in [outer space](/wiki/Outer_space "Outer space"), on other worlds, in [subterranean earth](/wiki/Hollow_Earth "Hollow Earth"),[[280]](#cite_note-DK-2015-280) or in [parallel universes](/wiki/Parallel_universe_(fiction)#Science_fiction "Parallel universe (fiction)");[[281]](#cite_note-britannica-281)
* [Fictional concepts in biology](/wiki/Biology_in_fiction "Biology in fiction") such as [aliens](/wiki/Extraterrestrial_life "Extraterrestrial life"), [mutants](/wiki/Mutants_in_fiction "Mutants in fiction"), and [enhanced humans](/wiki/Enhanced_human "Enhanced human");[[278]](#cite_note-GW-278)[[282]](#cite_note-Parker-282)
* Undiscovered scientific possibilities such as [teleportation](/wiki/Teleportation "Teleportation"), [time travel](/wiki/Time_travel "Time travel"), and [faster-than-light](/wiki/Faster-than-light "Faster-than-light") travel or [communication](/wiki/Ansible "Ansible");[[283]](#cite_note-283)
* Social/political systems and situations that are new and different, including [utopian](/wiki/Utopian "Utopian"),[[280]](#cite_note-DK-2015-280) [dystopian](/wiki/Dystopia "Dystopia"), [post-apocalyptic](/wiki/Post-apocalyptic "Post-apocalyptic"), or [post-scarcity](/wiki/Post-scarcity "Post-scarcity");[[284]](#cite_note-284)
* [Future history](/wiki/Future_history "Future history") and [speculative evolution](/wiki/Speculative_evolution "Speculative evolution") of humans on Earth or other planets;[[285]](#cite_note-Ashley-285)
* [Paranormal](/wiki/Paranormal "Paranormal") abilities such as [mind control](/wiki/Mind_control_in_popular_culture "Mind control in popular culture"), [telepathy](/wiki/Telepathy "Telepathy"), and [telekinesis](/wiki/Telekinesis "Telekinesis").[[286]](#cite_note-286)
